patches.summary: Add Shape (c) to extractStreamValues for Strava's key_by_type=true response format.
patches.reason: When activities/drift and training/zones call GET /streams?key_by_type=true, Strava returns {"heartrate":{"type":"heartrate","data":[...]}} — a nested map, not a flat array. The original two-branch implementation silently returned nil for this shape, making drift always emit empty output.
patches.validated_outcome: extractStreamValues now handles all three Strava stream shapes: single-stream {data:[...]}, flat keyed {heartrate:[...]}, and keyed-object {heartrate:{type:...,data:[...]}}.

patches.summary: Fix bulk-update inter-request sleep from 100ms to 4500ms.
patches.reason: Strava's limit is 200 req/15 min = 4.5 s/req minimum for sustained bulk operations. The original 100ms sleep (a 45x underestimate) would exhaust the quota on any non-trivial batch, triggering cascading 429s.

patches.summary: Handle both flat and nested segment schemas in segments_kom_gap query.
patches.reason: Synced 'segments' rows store flat {name, distance} while 'starred' rows store a nested {segment: {name, distance}} object. Querying both types with flat field access silently produced unnamed 0-km segments for all starred rows.

patches.summary: Add 500ms sleep between per-gear GET /gear/{id} calls in gear status.
patches.reason: Unbounded sequential API calls with no sleep could silently exhaust Strava's shared 200 req/15 min quota when a user has many gear items.

patches.summary: Repoint power-curve and training-zones stream queries to resources table (resource_type='streams').
patches.reason: Both commands JOINed on the activities_streams typed table, which sync never populates. Individual stream fetches write to the generic resources table under resource_type='streams'. Repointed queries to resources JOIN on id. Added actionable error when no stream data is cached instead of silent all-zero output.
patches.validated_outcome: Commands now query the correct table and return an error with instructions (run 'activities streams get-activity <id>') when no streams are cached.

patches.summary: Add full pagination loop to segments_progress (was silently truncating at 200 efforts).
patches.reason: per_page is capped at 200 (Strava's max). Athletes with >200 efforts on a segment had the tail silently dropped, giving a wrong PR and an incomplete history. Added a page-loop that stops when Strava returns an empty page; --limit still caps early.
